etymology.description: 電(電) (diàn, "electric") + 話(話) (huà, "speech") form 'telephone'; 號(號) (hào, "number") + 碼(碼) (mǎ, "code") form 'number'. A 電話(電話) (diànhuà, "telephone") lets speech travel via electricity. A 號碼(號碼) (hàomǎ, "number") is given to connect calls.
etymology.interpretation: 電(電) (diàn, "electric") + 話(話) (huà, "speech"): talking through electricity makes long-distance communication possible, thus 'telephone'. 號(號) (hào, "number") + 碼(碼) (mǎ, "code"): a precise digit sequence or 'code' is necessary for dialing, hence 'number' in phone contexts.

examples.chinese: 你能给我你的电话号码吗(你能給我你的電話號碼嗎)？
examples.pinyin: Nǐ néng gěi wǒ nǐ de diànhuà hàomǎ ma?
examples.english: Can you give me your telephone number?

examples.chinese: 我错记了他的电话号码(我錯記了他的電話號碼)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ cuòjì le tā de diànhuà hàomǎ.

examples.chinese: 我们联系的方式是通过电子邮件和电话号码(我們聯繫的方式是通過電子郵件和電話號碼)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen liánxì de fāngshì shì tōngguò diànzǐ yóujiàn hé diànhuà hàomǎ.
examples.english: Our ways of communication are through email and telephone number.
